meaning: vt.选举,推选v.选择,作出选择,选举,选举,选举
examples: The committee voted to re-elect him (as) chairman.
Councils should be answerable to the people who elect them.
Increasing numbers of people elect to work from home nowadays.
Woodrow Wilson, a Calvinist, believed he was one of the elect, chosen to lead, teach and inspire.
